enforced.mr_name: canonicalize RNEXT between explicit and '=' alias preserves semantics
enforced.oracle: The output SAM file, when parsed and re-serialized by a conformant SUT, must produce identical alignment records (with RNEXT either as '=' or explicit RNAME depending on canonicalization direction) as the original file.

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.oracle: The output SAM file, when parsed and re-serialized by a conformant SUT, must produce identical alignment records (with SEQ in uppercase) as the original file processed through the same SUT pipeline.

evidence.quote: No assumptions can be made on the letter cases.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR query-consuming length mismatch with SEQ length
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field order permutation on alignment records
quarantine.oracle: After permuting the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields on alignment lines, the SUT must produce identical biological interpretation (same alignments, same tags, same values). Round-trip invariance: the file content after normalization should be equivalent.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @HD header subtag field order permutation
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs inside the @HD header line, the SUT must produce identical biological interpretation. The order of fields within a header line is not significant per spec.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote about field order non-significance is from the alignment optional fields section (p54), but the same principle applies to header lines per p26/p83 which state 'Within each (non-@CO) header line...the order in which the fields appear is not significant' — though that chunk was above threshold.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @SQ header record subtag field order permutation
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @SQ header line, the SUT must produce identical biological interpretation. The order of fields within a header line is not significant per spec.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote about field order non-significance is from the alignment optional fields section; the header section (p83) states the same principle for header lines but was above threshold.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @RG header record subtag field order permutation
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @RG header line, the SUT must produce identical biological interpretation. The order of fields within a header line is not significant per spec.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote about field order non-significance is from the alignment optional fields section; the header section (p83) states the same principle for header lines but was above threshold.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM @PG header record subtag field order permutation
quarantine.oracle: After shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @PG header line, the SUT must produce identical biological interpretation. The order of fields within a header line is not significant per spec.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec quote about field order non-significance is from the alignment optional fields section; the header section (p83) states the same principle for header lines but was above threshold.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of @CO comment lines in the header, the SUT must produce identical biological interpretation. @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec distinguishes @CO lines from other header lines by noting they don't follow TAG:VALUE format, implying they are free-text comments. The SAM normalizer sorts them, confirming no ordering semantics.

quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ and @RG header lines among themselves (keeping @HD first), the SUT must produce identical biological interpretation. The spec only mandates @HD be first; other record types have no required order.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec explicitly requires @HD to be the first line if present. For @SQ lines, coordinate sort order is defined by @SQ line order, so reordering @SQ lines changes coordinate sort semantics — this MR is only valid for unsorted/queryname-sorted files.

quarantine.mr_name: split or merge adjacent CIGAR ops preserves semantics
quarantine.oracle: The output SAM file, when parsed and re-serialized by a conformant SUT, must produce identical alignment records (with equivalent CIGAR representation) as the original file processed through the same SUT pipeline.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly forbid adjacent identical CIGAR operations, but the SAM Recommended Practice suggests they should be different. Splitting/merging preserves the total consumed lengths.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: After removing zero-length CIGAR ops and merging newly-adjacent identical ops, the alignment semantics (POS, RNAME, SEQ, QUAL, tags) must be preserved, and the total consumed query and reference lengths must remain unchanged.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly forbid zero-length CIGAR ops; the recommendation is ADVISORY, so both forms are valid SAM.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR M to =/X split normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: After rewriting M ops to =/X using the MD tag and recomputing NM, the alignment must be semantically identical: POS, RNAME, SEQ, QUAL, and all other tags must remain unchanged, and the new NM tag must equal the original NM value.

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: The spec allows M as a synonym for =/X; the MD tag is required for the transform, and not all alignments carry it.

quarantine.mr_name: Flag bit 0x4 set on mapped record with non-star RNAME and positive POS
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'no assumptions can be made' when 0x4 is set, which is permissive; the Recommended Practice uses 'should' (advisory) rather than 'must'. Some parsers may accept the contradiction silently.

quarantine.mr_name: Illegal optional tag type character outside AifZHB
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: In an optional field, TYPE is a single case-sensitive letter which defines the format of VALUE

ambiguity_flags: The spec lists valid types (A, i, f, Z, H, B) but does not explicitly say 'MUST NOT use other letters' — it's implied by the definition. Some lenient parsers may silently skip unrecognized tag types.

quarantine.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired-end template
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH; silent acceptance or SILENT_SKIP is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: TLEN & Int & $[-2^31+1,\,2^31-1]$ & observed Template LENgth

ambiguity_flags: The spec defines TLEN as 'observed Template LENgth' with signed integer range but the exact opposite-sign convention for paired reads is implementation-defined per p51/p119. Some parsers may not validate TLEN sign consistency.

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with SQ LN bound check preserves coordinate consistency
quarantine.oracle: After shifting all POS values by +shift and increasing @SQ LN by the same shift, the invariant POS + sum(ref-consuming CIGAR ops) <= LN must hold for all mapped records, and the relative ordering of records by coordinate must be preserved.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: PNEXT: 1-based Position of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. Set as 0 when the information is unavailable.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be unmapped' (ADVISORY), not 'must be unmapped' (CRITICAL), so some SUTs may accept out-of-bounds alignments.

quarantine.mr_name: Unmapped record field normalization preserves coordinate semantics
quarantine.oracle: After normalizing MAPQ to 255 for unmapped reads (FLAG 0x4), the coordinate fields (RNAME, POS) remain unchanged and the record is still valid. The 1-based coordinate system is preserved.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

evidence.quote: PNEXT: 1-based Position of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. Set as 0 when the information is unavailable.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be set' (ADVISORY) for RNAME/POS of unmapped reads, not 'must be set'.
